# Devlog 36: Alternative Memory Architectures for Math (2026-03-18 → 2026-03-19)

*Generated by research-copilot*

## Overview

Explored whether fundamentally different memory architectures could help on math, following the headroom search (devlog 34) which showed technique-level concepts are structurally mismatched with math failure modes.

Tested three approaches: episodic memory (worked solutions), context warm-up (any solution), and TF-IDF retrieval. All null on math at powered sample sizes.

## Approaches Tested

| Approach | Mechanism | N | Result |
|---|---|---|---|
| Episodic (relevant worked solution) | TF-IDF retrieval, inject similar solved problem | 20 | +5pp (noise — random control matched) |
| Context warm-up (any worked solution) | Inject random solved problem | 108 | 0pp (null) |
| Problem-only injection | Inject random problem statement, no solution | 108 | 0pp (null) |

## Key Findings

### 1. Random control killed the episodic hypothesis (n=20)
The smoketest showed +5pp with episodic memory. Adding a random-example control (inject a random, not similar, worked solution) showed the same +5pp. The benefit was from extra context, not retrieval relevance.

### 2. The context effect was noise (n=108)
At n=108 (12 per difficulty d1-d9), all three conditions were within 2pp of baseline (67.6%): math-warmup 65.7%, problem-only 66.7%. The smoketest's +5pp was a single-problem flip at n=20 (p=0.50 by McNemar's test).

### 3. Retrieval quality audit
TF-IDF retrieval is viable for formulaic problem types (recurrences sim > 0.7, functional equations > 0.6) but poor for geometry (< 0.3) and constructive proofs (< 0.2). Self-retrieval within Omni-MATH (mean sim 0.485) is much better than cross-corpus from Math L5 (mean sim 0.330).

## Methodological Lessons

- **Always include a random-example control** when testing retrieval/selection. Without it, we would have scaled the episodic pipeline before discovering the null.
- **Do not conclude from n<50.** The +5pp at n=20 was a coin flip.
- **Progressive sample sizes work:** retrieval audit ($0) → smoketest ($1) → random control ($0.50) → powered experiment ($3). Total cost to close the direction: ~$5.

## Files Created
- `scripts/retrieval_quality_audit.py` — TF-IDF retrieval quality check
- `scripts/episodic_smoketest.py` — 3-condition episodic memory test
- `scripts/warmup_experiment.py` — 3-condition powered warm-up test (d1-d9)
- `docs/35_retrieval_quality_audit_2026_03_18.md` — TF-IDF audit devlog
